page: Microsoft Azure NV32as_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the NVSv4 server family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 112.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB of RAM per core.

This virtual machine includes a single bundled GPU accelerator and features 721 GB of local storage. Additionally, the instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach high-performance external storage volumes to meet demanding input/output requirements. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the NV32as_v4 offers a balanced resource density by pairing a dedicated AMD processor with a single GPU and moderate memory capacity. This configuration is engineered for workloads requiring GPU acceleration alongside multi-threaded CPU processing, such as virtual desktop infrastructure, hardware-accelerated rendering, and entry-level machine learning tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure NV32as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the NVSv4 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 112.0 GB of system memory, a single bundled GPU, and 721 GB of local storage. The instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external storage attachments, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the NV32as_v4 provides a balanced allocation of compute, memory, and graphics acceleration. This hardware profile makes it suitable for workloads requiring GPU acceleration and multi-threaded CPU capabilities, such as virtual desktop hosting, 3D rendering, and moderate parallel processing tasks. The system delivers 3.5 GB of RAM per core, optimizing resource density for graphics-intensive applications and parallel computing tasks.

tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated cores, a bundled graphics accelerator, and premium storage capabilities for parallel workloads.
